title: Indulgent Smoky Middle Eastern Egg Mansaf
description: A silky, smoky egg dish served on a cedar-plank, blending Middle Eastern flavors with a midnight-craving twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius and place a cedar-plank inside to heat.
In a small skillet, warm the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the minced garlic until fragrant, about one minute.
In a bowl, whisk together the yogurt, smoked paprika, liquid smoke, smoked sea salt, black pepper, parsley, dried mint, katsuramuki cut dill, and cilantro until smooth.
Brush the heated cedar-plank with a thin layer of olive oil and spread the yogurt mixture evenly across its surface.
Carefully crack the six eggs onto the prepared plank, spacing them evenly so they stay separate.
Transfer the plank to the oven and bake for 12 minutes, or until the egg whites are set and the yolks remain slightly runny.
Remove the cedar-plank from the oven and let the eggs rest for two minutes before serving.
Plate the smoky eggs, garnish with extra fresh herbs if desired, and serve immediately.
